title: 第 38 節
en: 1 The chief object is to show the different ways on which people proceed after death. One of these ways, the Devapatha that leads to Brahman and from which there is no return, has been described, IV, 15. The other ways for those who on earth know the conditioned Brahman only, have to be discussed now.

2 The same fable, the pra7zasa;/zvada or pra;zavidya, is told in the Br/hadara«yaka VI, 1, 1-14, the Aitareya Ar. II, 4, the Kaush. Up. Ill, 3, and the Prama Up. II, 3. The last is the simplest version of all, but it does not follow therefore that it is the oldest. It would be difficult to find two fables apparently more alike, yet in reality differing from each other more characteristically than this fable and the fable told to the plebeians by Menenius Agrippa.

V PRAPATFAKA, I RUANDA, 12. 73 7. They went to their father Pra^apati and said : ' Sir, who is the best of us ?' He replied : ' He by whose departure the body seems worse than worst, he is the best of you.'

8. The tongue (speech) departed, and having been absent for a year, it came round and said : ' How have you been able to live without me V They replied : ' Like mute people, not speaking, but breathing with the breath, seeing with the eye, hearing with the ear, thinking with the mind. Thus we lived/ Then speech went back.

9. The eye (sight) departed, and having been absent for a year, it came round and said : ' How have you been able to live without me ?' They replied : ' Like blind people, not seeing, but breathing with the breath, speaking with the tongue, hearing with the ear, thinking with the mind. Thus we lived.' Then the eye went back.

10. The ear (hearing) departed, and having been absent for a year, it came round and said : ' How have you been able to live without me?' They replied : ' Like deaf people, not hearing, but breathing with the breath, speaking with the tongue, thinking with the mind. Thus we lived/ Then the ear went back.

11. The mind departed, and having been absent for a year, it came round and said : ' How have you been able to live without me ?' They replied : ' Like children whose mind is not yet formed, but breathing with the breath, speaking with the tongue, seeing with the eye, hearing with the ear. Thus we lived.' Then the mind went back.
12. The breath, when on the point of departing, tore up the other senses, as a horse, going to start, 74 tftfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.
might tear up the pegs to which he is tethered 1. They came to him and said : ' Sir, be thou (our lord) ; thou art the best among us. Do not depart from us !'
13. Then the tongue said to him : ' If I am the richest, thou art the richest.' The eye said to him : ' If I am the firm rest, thou art the firm rest2.'
14. The ear said to him : ' If I am success, thou art success.' The mind said to him : ' If I am the home, thou art the home.'
15. And people do not call them, the tongues, the eyes, the ears, the minds, but the breaths (pra;£a, the senses). For breath are all these.
Second Khanda.

1. Breath said: 'What shall be my food?' They answered : ' Whatever there is, even unto dogs and birds.' Therefore this is food for Ana (the breather). His name is clearly Ana3. To him who knows this there is nothing that is not (proper) food.

2. He said: 'What shall be my dress?' They answered : ' Water.' Therefore wise people, when they are going to eat food, surround their food before and after with water4. He (pra;/a) thus gains a dress, and is no longer naked 5.
1 Pa^/vua, fetter, nedrj, pedica, a word now well known, but which Burnouf (Commentaire sur le Yacna, Notes, CLXXIV) tried in vain to decipher.
2 Burnouf rightly preferred pratish/Msi to pratish//zo 'si, though the commentary on the corresponding passage of the B/-/hadarawyaka seems to favour tatpratish//$o 'si.
3 Ana, breather, more general than pra-ana = pra«a, forth breather, and the other more specified names of breath.
4 They rinse the mouth before and after every meal.

'' We expect, 'He who knows this' instead of prawa, but as V PRAPATOAKA, 2 KHAiVDA, 6. 75 3. Satyakama 6"abala, after he had communicated this to Gomiti Vaiyaghrapadya, said to him : ' If you were to tell this to a dry stick, branches would grow, and leaves spring from it.'

4. If * a man wishes to reach greatness, let him perform the Diksha 2 (preparatory rite) on the day of the new moon, and then, on the night of the full moon, let him stir a mash of all kinds of herbs with curds and honey, and let him pour ghee on the fire (avasathya laukika), saying, ' Svaha to the oldest and the best.' After that let him throw all that remains (of the ghee)3 into the mash.
5. In the same manner let him pour ghee on the fire, saying, ' Svaha to the richest.' After that let him throw all that remains together into the mash.
In the same manner let him pour ghee on the fire, saying, ' Svaha to the firm rest.' After that let him throw all that remains together into the mash.
In the same manner let him pour ghee on the fire, saying, ' Svaha to success.' After that let him throw all that remains together into the mash.
6. Then going forward and placing the mash pra;/a may apply to every individual pra«a, the usual finishing sentence was possibly dropt on purpose.
1 The oblation here described is called mantha, a mortar, or what is pounded in a mortar, i.e. barley stirred in some kind of gravy. See Gaim. N. M. V. p. 406.

2 Not the real diksha, which is a preparatory rite for great sacrifices, but penance, truthfulness, abstinence, which take the place of diksha with those who live in the forest and devote themselves to upasana, meditative worship.
3 What is here called sampatam avanayati is the same as sawsravam avanayati in the B/v'h. Ar. VI, 3, 2. The commentator says : Sruvavalepanam agy&m mantham sa7/;sravayati.
76 A'tfANDOGYA-UPANISHAD.

in his hands, he recites: 'Thou (Pra«a) art Ama1 by name, for all this together exists in thee. He is the oldest and best, the king, the sovereign. May he make me the oldest, the best, the king, the sovereign. May I be all this.'
zh: 主要目的是展示人死後所循的不同道路。其中一條道路——通往梵天（Brahman）的提婆之路（Devapatha），從此不復返，已於第四卷第十五節論述過。現在須討論另外的道路，即那些在世間僅認識制約梵天之人所走的道路。

2 同一個寓言故事——吠陀知識（prajñavidya）或所謂的真知（prajñasavidya）——見於《婆羅多林迦奧義書》（Brhadaranyaka）VI, 1, 1-14、《愛達瑞亞梵書》（Aitareya Ar.）II, 4、《考什陀基奧義書》（Kaush. Up.）III, 3，以及《普剌濕那奧義書》（Prama Up.）II, 3。最後這個版本是所有版本中最簡樸的，但由此並不能推斷它是最古老的。很難找到兩個寓言故事看似更加相似，然而實質上卻相異得更為深刻的例子，比起這個寓言故事與梅尼紐斯‧阿格里帕（Menenius Agrippa）講給平民百姓的寓言故事之間的關係。
第五卷，普拉帕蒂卡，盧安達，12‧73 他們來到他們的父親普拉帕蒂（Pra^apati），說道：「尊者啊，我們之中誰是最好的呢？」他回答說：「那個人若是離開了，身體就會顯得比最壞還要更壞，他就是你們之中最好的。」
8. 舌頭（言語）離開了，缺席了一年之後，它回來說：「你們沒有我怎麼能活著呢？」他們回答：「像啞巴一樣，不說話，但用呼吸呼吸，用眼睛看，用耳朵聽，用心智思考。就這樣我們活著。」於是言語又回去了。
9. 眼睛（視覺）離去了，缺席一年後，它回來說：「你們沒有我怎麼活的？」他們回答：「像盲人一樣，看不到東西，但我們用呼吸來呼吸，用舌頭說話，用耳朵聽，用心智思考。我們就這樣活著。」於是眼睛就離開了。
10. 耳朵（聽覺）離開了，缺席一年後，它回來說：「你們沒有我怎麼活得下去？」他們回答：「就像聾子一樣，聽不到，但用氣息呼吸，用舌頭說話，用心智思考。我們就這樣活著。」然後耳朵就回去了。
11. 心智離開了，缺席一年之後，它回來並說：「你們如何能夠沒有我而活著？」他們回答說：「如同心智尚未形成的孩童一般，我們以呼吸呼吸，以舌頭說話，以眼睛看見，以耳朵聽聞。我們就是這樣活著的。」然後心智就回去了。
12. 當生氣正要離去時，它如同一匹將要啟程的馬一樣，連根拔起其他的感官，74 《昌多吉亞奧義書》。
可能會拔起他所繫著的木樁1。他們來到他面前說：「先生，願你（做我們的主人）；你是我們當中最好的。不要離開我們！」
13. 於是舌頭對他說：『若我是最富有的，那麼你也是最富有的。』眼睛對他說：『若我是堅固的依託，那麼你也是堅固的依託²。』
14. 耳朵對他說：「若我是成功，汝亦是成功。」心意對他說：「若我是家宅，汝亦是家宅。」
15. 人們不是稱呼它們為舌頭、眼睛、耳朵、心靈，而是稱為呼吸（prāṇa，感官）。因為呼吸就是所有這一切。
第二章
呼吸說：「我的食物將是什麼？」他們回答：「凡有之物，甚至狗與鳥。」因此這是阿那（呼吸者）的食物。他的名字明白是阿那。對於知道這一點的人而言，沒有什麼不是（恰當的）食物。
2. 他說：「我的衣著應該是什麼？」他們回答：「水。」因此智慧之人在進食時，於食物的前後都用水來圍繞它^4。他（prajāpati）因此獲得衣著，不再赤身露體^5。
帕瓦（Pavua）、束縛（fetter）、涅德日（nedrj）、佩迪卡（pedica），這是一個現已廣為人知的詞彙，但布爾努夫（Commentaire sur le Yacna, Notes, CLXXIV）曾徒勞地試圖破譯它。
布爾努夫（Burnouf）恰當地偏好 pratish/Msi 勝於 pratish//zo 'si，儘管《卜路奧帕尼沙奧義書》（B/-/hadarawyaka）相應段落的註疏似乎傾向於 tatpratish//$o 'si。
3 Ana，呼吸者，比 pra-ana = pra-āṇa（向外呼吸者）以及其他更具體的呼吸名稱更為通泛。
他們在每餐前後都要漱口。
我們預期應當是「知此者」（He who knows this），而非 prawa，但見《梨俱吠陀》第二篇第六章第七十五節（VEDA, 2 KHANDA, 6. 75）。薩提亞卡摩（Satyakama）戈毗塔拉（Gobila）在將此傳授給戈彌蒂‧韋亞格拉帕德亞（Gomiti Vaiyaghrapadya）之後，對他說：「若你將此告知一根枯木，枝條必將生長，葉片亦將萌發而出。」
4. 如果一個人希望達到偉大，就讓他在新月之日進行迪克沙（Diksha）²（準備儀式），然後在滿月之夜，讓他用凝乳和蜂蜜攪拌各種草藥的混合物，並讓他在火上澆淋淨油（avasathya laukika），說「向最古老者和最好者致禮」（Svaha）。之後，讓他將所有剩餘的淨油³倒入混合物中。
5. 同樣地，讓他將酥油倒在火上，說道：「Svaha 敬獻予最富有者。」之後，讓他將所有剩餘的東西一併拋入糨糊中。
同樣地，讓他將酥油倒在火上，說道「吽（Svaha）敬獻於堅固的安住」。在此之後，讓他將所有剩餘的物質一起投入糊狀物中。
以同樣的方式，讓他在火上澆灑酥油（ghee），說著「願成功歸於汝」（Svaha to success）。之後，讓他將所有剩餘的東西一併投入麥芽漿中。
6. 然後向前進行，將糊狀物（mash pra）應用於每一個單獨的 pra，通常的結尾句子可能被刻意省略了。 --- **說明**：原文此句存在明顯的文本破損或印刷錯誤（「mash pra;/a」及 「pra«a」的符號異常），我依據文脈進行了最合理的推譯。若有更完整的版本或上下文，翻譯可進一步精化。
此處所述的獻祭稱為 mantha（研臼，或研臼中被搗碎的物質），即攪拌在某種肉汁中的大麥。見 Gaim. N. M. V. p. 406。
不是真正的入壇式（diksha），那是為盛大祭祀的準備儀式，而是懺悔、誠實與節制，這些對於住在森林中、專心於烏帕薩那（upasana）、即冥想崇拜的人而言，是用來取代入壇式的。
這裡稱為 sampatam avanayati 的，與《白驛俱吠陀》（Ṛg-Veda）第六卷第三章第二節中的 sawsravam avanayati 相同。註釋者說：Sruvavalepanam agy&m mantham sawsravayati。
76 阿特瓦那多吉亞奧義書 (Aitareya-Upanishad)
在他的手中，他誦念：「汝（生命氣 Prana）名為阿摩（Ama），蓋一切此物悉聚於汝。彼為最古老者、最善者、國王、主宰。願彼使我為最古老者、最善者、國王、主宰。願我為此一切。」
